keyP2IntegrationPoints: Nexus Public REST API (tenant-scoped, API key auth, OpenAPI spec needed)
The Citadel web portal (OAuth2 / session token, per-tenant namespace)
Per-client Telegram Bot (separate bot token per tenant)
Holonet Inbound Webhooks (HMAC verification, per-tenant URL namespace)
Holocron Knowledge API (per-client Notion folder scope)
Postgres RLS (SET LOCAL app.tenant_id — enforces all data isolation)
Docker container isolation (per-client filesystem, process, network, credentials)
p4PhysicalDeploymentRationale: OpenClaw runs natively on Mac Mini M-series hardware. Gemma4:26b on Mac Mini M4 Pro 48GB covers all regulated workloads with zero data residency risk. Platform is configuration-file-driven, containerisable via Docker, and Tailscale-connectable for AInchors remote management. All APRA CPG 234/235 controls (immutable audit log, AES-256, TLS 1.3, WORM archive) implementable on self-hosted infrastructure. Minimum viable P4 stack defined: 2x Mac Mini M4 Pro + NAS + Tailscale + Postgres TDE + pgvector + Gemma4 + Sanctum + Warden.
openDecisionsForKen: A — Multi-tenancy isolation: Shared schema + RLS from day one (recommended) vs Schema-per-tenant (interim option)
B — P4 deployment model: Consulting engagement (recommended) vs Packaged installation (deferred Year 3+)
C — Holocron at P4 FSI: Hybrid (Notion internal + self-hosted client KB) vs Notion-restrictions-only
D — The Citadel build approach: Notion-based P2 / custom build P4 (recommended) vs direct custom build from P2
E — Holonet P2 scope: REST/webhooks only (recommended) vs include DB connectors
F — Warden deployment: AInchors-managed P2 (recommended) vs per-tenant from P2
G — P3 commercial tier trigger: Formal ROI checklist required before any P3 unlock — Ken approves
H — Anthropic DPA: Block Claude for all P2 client workloads at launch; pursue DPA verification as Q1 priority
